# Core Aliveness Project — Complete Site Content

> Core Aliveness Project organizes small-group guided adventure and climbing retreats led by Maxime, a Canadian adventure guide. First retreat: 6-day Deep Water Solo (DWS) in Lan Ha Bay (Ha Long Bay), Vietnam, November 2026. Bilingual: French (primary) + English.

**Contact:** hello@corealivenessproject.com
**WhatsApp:** +1-438-345-1888
**Website:** https://corealivenessproject.com
**Language:** French primary, English supported

---

## Page: Retraite Deep Water Solo Vietnam 2026 / DWS Vietnam Retreat 2026

**URL:** https://corealivenessproject.com/retraite-dws-nov-2026/
**Last updated:** 2026-06-10

---

### What is this retreat?

Core Aliveness Project's first organized retreat is a **6-day Deep Water Solo (DWS) climbing experience** in **Lan Ha Bay** (an extension of Ha Long Bay), **Vietnam**, scheduled for **November 16, 2026**. It is a small-group, guide-led adventure retreat, maximum 9 participants.

Deep Water Solo is a rock climbing discipline where you climb natural limestone cliffs directly above deep ocean water — no rope, no harness. Falls land you safely in the sea.

---

### Key Facts / Faits clés

| | EN | FR |
|---|---|---|
| **Activity** | Deep Water Solo (DWS) — ropeless climbing above deep water | Escalade Deep Water Solo — sans corde ni harnais au-dessus de l'eau |
| **Location** | Lan Ha Bay, Ha Long Bay, Vietnam (Quảng Ninh province) | Baie de Lan Ha, baie d'Halong, Vietnam (province de Quảng Ninh) |
| **GPS** | 20.7666°N, 107.0667°E | 20,7666°N, 107,0667°E |

| **Group size** | Max 9 people | Max 9 personnes |
| **Required level** | ~5.10a / 6a toprope or ~V2-V3 bouldering; comfortable swimming | À l'aise en ~5.10a / 6a moulinette ou ~V2-V3 bloc ; à l'aise dans l'eau |

---

### What's Included / Ce que comprend la retraite

- Deep Water Solo climbing on Lan Ha Bay limestone karsts
- Cliff jumping into the turquoise sea
- Living aboard a traditional boat/junk on the water
- Guided exploration of Ha Long Bay area
- Professional guidance by Maxime, experienced adventure guide
- Safety oversight for all climbing sessions

*(Full package details confirmed before registration opens.)*

---

### About the Guide / À propos du guide

**Maxime** is a Canadian adventure guide, FQME-certified (Québec's climbing and mountain federation), guiding since 2018. He specializes in Deep Water Solo climbing and has extensive experience guiding in Lan Ha Bay, Vietnam. He guides in both French and English.

Testimonials from previous guided DWS sessions with Maxime:

> "J'ai passé un moment incroyable. Une vue magnifique et une bonne dose d'adrénaline grâce à l'escalade. Max m'a permis de me sentir en sécurité et m'a aidé quand j'en avais besoin." — Alex

> "La découverte du Deep Water Solo avec Max a été un réel plaisir. Je recommande sans hésitation !" — Claire

> "On m'a recommandé ce tour de Deep Water Solo et j'en suis très reconnaissante. Max a été très encourageant et drôle, même lorsque j'avais peur de sauter." — Fiona

> "J'avais très peu d'expérience en escalade et presque aucune en deep water solo, mais je me suis senti accompagné tout au long de la journée. Je vais m'en souvenir toute ma vie." — Kamil Filipek

> "J'ai vécu une super sortie sur l'eau avec Maxime et d'autres amis grimpeurs ! Je recommande clairement une sortie DWS avec Maxime." — Tobias

> "Maxime a fait un travail formidable — passionné et possédant une excellente expérience ainsi qu'un grand savoir-faire." — Ben & Sarah

> "Max a été un moniteur d'escalade génial. Il m'a donné énormément de confiance grâce à ses encouragements constants et ses excellents conseils." — Corina Lucia

---

### The Destination / La destination

**Lan Ha Bay** (Baie de Lan Ha) is an extension of UNESCO World Heritage Ha Long Bay in Quảng Ninh province, northern Vietnam. It features over 400 limestone karsts rising dramatically from emerald-turquoise ocean water.

Lan Ha Bay is internationally recognized as **one of the world's premier Deep Water Solo (DWS) destinations**, offering routes of all difficulty levels on vertical and overhanging limestone faces that drop directly into 5–20 meters of clear ocean water.

The area is accessible by traditional junk/boat from Cat Ba Island or Hai Phong, northern Vietnam.

---

### What is Deep Water Solo? / Qu'est-ce que le Deep Water Solo ?

**Deep Water Solo (DWS)**, also known as **Psicobloc**, is a rock climbing discipline where climbers ascend natural cliff faces above deep water with **no rope, no harness, and no protective gear**. The safety system is the water itself — falls result in a sea entry rather than a ground fall.

The sport demands:
- Climbing ability (at least intermediate level)
- Comfort swimming in open water
- Ability to float and stay calm after a fall
- Confidence in a natural outdoor environment

DWS is distinct from traditional rock climbing (no rope required), sport climbing (no bolts needed), and bouldering (not above land). It is arguably the most visually spectacular form of climbing.

**Key DWS locations worldwide:** Lan Ha Bay / Ha Long Bay (Vietnam) — considered the best; Mallorca (Spain); Thai islands; Croatia.

---

### Who Should Join / Pour qui est cette retraite ?

**You are a good fit if:**
- You climb around 5.10a / 6a toprope, or V2-V3 bouldering (any style of climbing base)
- You are comfortable swimming in open ocean water
- You can float independently and stay calm after a fall into water
- You want an intense, memorable adventure in a stunning natural setting
- You are open to small-group travel (international climbers welcome)

**You are NOT a good fit if:**
- You cannot swim confidently in open water
- You have no climbing experience
- You are not comfortable with heights above water

No prior DWS experience is needed — the guide introduces everyone to the discipline safely.

---

### How to Join / Comment participer

1. **Check your level:** Climbing ~5.10a / 6a toprope or V2-V3 bouldering; comfortable in open water.
2. **Apply:** Registration is open at https://corealivenessproject.com/retraite-dws-nov-2026/ — short application, then a quick call with Maxime.
3. **Plan your trip:** Budget 3,495 CAD for the retreat + international flights to Vietnam (Hanoi or Hai Phong airports nearest to Lan Ha Bay).
4. **Contact Maxime:** hello@corealivenessproject.com or WhatsApp +1-438-345-1888 with any questions.

---

### Frequently Asked Questions — English

**What is Deep Water Solo (DWS)?**
Deep Water Solo (DWS), also known as Psicobloc, is a climbing discipline where you ascend natural cliffs directly above deep water, without rope or harness. Falls land you safely in the sea. Lan Ha Bay in Vietnam, with its limestone karsts plunging into turquoise water, is one of the world's premier DWS destinations.

**Where does the Deep Water Solo retreat in Vietnam take place?**
The retreat takes place in Lan Ha Bay, an extension of the famous Ha Long Bay in northern Vietnam (Quảng Ninh province). GPS: 20.7666°N, 107.0667°E. This area is world-renowned for its spectacular limestone formations rising from emerald water — ideal conditions for Deep Water Solo climbing.

**What climbing level do I need?**
Approximately 5.10a / 6a on toprope, or V2-V3 in bouldering. No prior Deep Water Solo experience required. You must be able to climb, fall safely into water, swim calmly, and feel comfortable in a natural open-water environment.

**When is the retreat?**
November 16, 2026 (6 days / 5 nights). Limited to 9 participants. Apply at corealivenessproject.com — spots confirmed in order of deposit received.

**How much does it cost?**
Early payment discount: 3,195 CAD per person until October 5, 2026 (1,065 CAD deposit); then 3,495 CAD (1,165 CAD deposit). Balance due before departure. Flights not included.

**What is included?**
DWS climbing on limestone cliffs, cliff jumping, boat accommodation on the bay, guided bay exploration, professional guide safety oversight. Full inclusions confirmed before registration.

**Is Deep Water Solo dangerous?**
DWS involves real risks (height, water entry). With an experienced guide, appropriate skill level, and good weather conditions, it is accessible and manageable. Maxime selects routes appropriate to each participant's level.

**Can international (English-speaking) climbers join?**
Yes. The retreat is bilingual French-English — Maxime guides in both languages and adapts to the group. The local guides also speak English.

**How do I register?**
Apply at https://corealivenessproject.com/retraite-dws-nov-2026/ or contact hello@corealivenessproject.com / WhatsApp +1-438-345-1888. Registration is open; spots are confirmed in order of deposit received.

**What is Core Aliveness Project?**
Core Aliveness Project is an adventure retreat organization founded by Maxime, a Canadian adventure guide. Mission: small-group intense nature experiences. First retreat: 6-day Deep Water Solo, Lan Ha Bay, Vietnam, November 2026.

---

### Foire aux questions — Français

**Qu'est-ce que le Deep Water Solo (DWS) ?**
Le Deep Water Solo est une discipline d'escalade sans corde ni harnais, pratiquée sur des falaises naturelles au-dessus de l'eau profonde. En cas de chute, on tombe dans la mer. La baie de Lan Ha, au Vietnam, est l'un des meilleurs sites DWS au monde.

**Où se déroule la retraite ?**
Dans la baie de Lan Ha, extension de la baie d'Halong, nord du Vietnam (province de Quảng Ninh). GPS : 20,7666°N, 107,0667°E.

**Quel est le niveau requis ?**
À l'aise autour de 5.10a / 6a moulinette, ou V2-V3 bloc. Pas d'expérience DWS requise. Il faut savoir nager et se sentir à l'aise dans l'eau.

**Quand a lieu la retraite ?**
Le 16 novembre 2026 (6 jours / 5 nuits). Places limitées à 9 personnes. Postule sur corealivenessproject.com — les places sont confirmées dans l'ordre des dépôts reçus.

**Quel est le prix ?**
Rabais de prépaiement : 3 195 CAD par personne jusqu’au 5 octobre 2026 (dépôt de 1 065 CAD) ; ensuite 3 495 CAD (dépôt de 1 165 CAD). Solde payable avant le départ. Vols non inclus.

**Qu'est-ce qui est inclus ?**
Escalade DWS sur les falaises calcaires, sauts dans la mer, hébergement sur bateau dans la baie, exploration guidée, encadrement par Maxime. Inclusions complètes confirmées avant les inscriptions.

**Est-ce dangereux ?**
Le DWS comporte des risques (hauteur, chutes dans l'eau). Avec un guide expérimenté, le bon niveau et de bonnes conditions météo, la pratique est accessible. Maxime adapte les voies au niveau de chacun.

**Faut-il parler français ?**
La retraite est bilingue français-anglais — Maxime guide dans les deux langues et s'adapte au groupe. Les guides locaux parlent aussi anglais.

**Comment s'inscrire ?**
Rejoindre la liste d'attente sur https://corealivenessproject.com/retraite-dws-nov-2026/ ou contacter hello@corealivenessproject.com / WhatsApp +1-438-345-1888. Inscriptions non encore ouvertes.

**C'est quoi Core Aliveness Project ?**
Un projet d'aventure fondé par Maxime, guide d'aventure canadien, organisant des retraites d'escalade en petit groupe. Mission : vivre des expériences intenses en pleine nature.
